context: Tests whether react-ban-use-effect can encode the policy into lint rules while preserving a narrow mount-only wrapper exception and existing hook guardrails.

checklist.name: Import ban
checklist.description: ESLint blocks importing `useEffect` from `react` in normal application code, for example with `no-restricted-imports`

checklist.name: Namespace call ban
checklist.description: ESLint blocks `React.useEffect(...)` namespace calls in normal application code, for example with `no-restricted-syntax`

checklist.name: Wrapper exception scoped
checklist.description: The policy allows direct useEffect only in `src/hooks/useMountEffect.ts` or an equivalently narrow shared wrapper path

checklist.name: Mount wrapper narrow
checklist.description: `useMountEffect` exposes mount-only setup/cleanup behavior and does not pretend to solve dependency-aware synchronization

checklist.name: Hooks lint preserved
checklist.description: Existing `react-hooks/rules-of-hooks` and `react-hooks/exhaustive-deps` enforcement is preserved or strengthened, not disabled globally

checklist.name: No unrelated bans
checklist.description: The policy does not ban unrelated APIs such as `useLayoutEffect` or non-React effect systems unless requested

checklist.name: Message points to replacements
checklist.description: Lint messages direct developers toward declarative replacements or the approved wrapper rather than dependency-array tuning

checklist.name: Verification named
checklist.description: The report says to run lint and includes positive/negative checks for blocked imports, blocked namespace calls, and the allowed wrapper

checklist.name: Boundary explained
checklist.description: The report explains that the wrapper is only for external-system mount synchronization and not fetching, derived state, resets, or relaying user actions
